howItWorks.intro: This excavation cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Volume
rules.formula: Cubic Yards = (L x W x D) / 27
rules.explanation: Divide cubic feet by 27 to get cubic yards.

rules.name: Haul Volume
rules.formula: Haul CY = Excavated CY x 1.25 (swell factor)
rules.explanation: Loose soil takes up 25% more space than in-ground soil.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: How much does excavation cost per cubic yard?
faq.a: Typical excavation costs are $5-$10 per cubic yard for loose topsoil, $10-$15 for clay or compacted soil, and $20-$40 for rocky ground. These are digging-only costs; hauling adds $300-$500 per 10-yard truck load.

faq.q: What is the swell factor for excavated soil?
faq.a: Excavated soil expands 20-30% in volume (swell factor of 1.2-1.3) because it loosens when dug. A 100 cubic yard hole produces roughly 125 cubic yards of loose material to haul away.
